stemHtml: If the first aircraft to reach the scene of an accident is not a search and rescue aircraft, what action must it take until the arrival of the first search and rescue aircraft?

options.a: Leave the area to avoid hampering subsequent search and rescue activities.
options.b: Take charge of on-scene activities of all other arriving aircraft.
options.c: Overfly the wreckage every 10 minutes, searching for any surface-to-air visual signals.
options.d: Remain in the area, overflying the scene of the accident at a height of 1000 ft on a North-South heading.
